key_insight: SciReasoner is a multimodal foundation model that preserves native structural information while reasoning transparently across proteins, molecules, and crystals to improve structure-property predictions.
clinical_relevance: Although not a cancer-drug-specific model, its stronger protein and chemistry reasoning could support target annotation, retrosynthesis, and early-stage drug discovery workflows.
method: Discretizes coordinates, topologies, and periodic connectivities into a unified structure-aware vocabulary.
Treats structural tokens as addressable evidence units during model reasoning.
Uses multimodal scientific foundation learning across proteins, small molecules, and inorganic crystals.
evidence_signal: Across 86 benchmarks, the model achieved state-of-the-art results on 67 tasks, and expert reviewers preferred or rated its reasoning traces comparable to a frontier LLM in 98% of cases.
watch_item: Performance gains may not directly transfer to cancer-specific discovery settings without additional validation on disease-relevant datasets and experimental follow-up.
paper_title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning
